growth_records.strain: Reactor M (mixotrophic, untreated inoculum). Preselected mixed culture from high-productivity source MES reactors (Cabau-Peinado et al. 2024 inoculum); inoculum dominated by Eubacterium, enriched over run toward Clostridium sensu stricto 12, Sporomusa, Oscillibacter
growth_records.medium: MES phosphate-buffered mineral medium + trace elements + sugars/peptone (Stage 1 mixotrophic catholyte)

up.method: TOC/HPLC concentration decline, 5-day co-consumption average (continuous phase 0.88 g/L/d)
concentration decline, 5-day co-consumption average (continuous phase 0.50 g/L/d)

sec.method: GC-FID; reactor M reached 11.2 g/L acetate by day 21; 3.9 g/L in first 5 days

sec.units: g/L/d (rose from 0.06 to 0.15 g/L/d; final 0.82 g/L)
g/L (final concentration; production rate ~0.04 g/L/d rising)

yields.note: carbon selectivity to acetate/butyrate/caproate/biomass reported qualitatively; coulombic efficiency of methane <1%

growth_records.cite: Ferretti, Jacopo et al. A two-stage strategy for methanogenesis suppression and rapid acetogenic biofilm formation in microbial electrosynthesis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2025.1655259. [PMC12616864]
growth_records.snippet: Acetate concentrations of 11.2 g L-1 in reactor M ... reached by day 21. Butyrate production rate in reactor M remained around 0.06 g L-1 d-1 ... rising to 0.15 g L-1 d-1. Reactor M reached a final [caproate] concentration of 1.49 g L-1.
growth_records.notes: Mixed undefined enriched prokaryotic community (acetogenic), NOT a single strain; volumetric productivities in g/L/d, biomass on electrode (measured as ~12.20 mmol C cm-3) so mmol/gDW/h conversion not possible. mu ~0.01 d-1 in continuous Stage 1 phase (planktonic, near-washout): growth_rate_per_h = 0.01/24 = 4.17e-4 /h; doubling_time_h = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.01 = 69.3 d = 1663 h. Uptake glucose/fructose reported as across all four reactors.

growth_records.medium: MES phosphate-buffered mineral medium + trace elements + sugars/peptone (Stage 1 heterotrophic catholyte, no polarisation/CO2)

sec.units: g/L (peak concentration, end of Stage 1; lower but more consistent rate)
g/L (final concentration; up to 0.6 g/L during Stage 1)

growth_records.cite: Ferretti, Jacopo et al. A two-stage strategy for methanogenesis suppression and rapid acetogenic biofilm formation in microbial electrosynthesis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2025.1655259. [PMC12616864]
growth_records.snippet: Acetate production rates in the heterotrophic reactor H were lower but more consistent throughout Stage 1 ... concentrations peaking towards the end of Stage 1 at 1.6 g L-1. Butyrate and caproate were also produced in reactor H, up to 0.05
growth_records.notes: Mixed undefined community, heterotrophic Stage 1. Volumetric concentrations/rates in g/L; no biomass-specific rate derivable (electrode biofilm). Caproate final 0.89 g/L (vs 1.49 in M). mu ~0.01 d-1 continuous phase.

growth_records.strain: Reactor HT (heterotrophic, heat-treated inoculum 120 C for 2 h)
growth_records.medium: MES phosphate-buffered mineral medium + trace elements + sugars/peptone (Stage 1 heterotrophic catholyte)

up.method: concentration decline, 5-day average (all reactors); faster in first 2 days for heat-treated

sec.units: g/L/d (rate throughout Stage 1; max concentration 0.8 g/L)

growth_records.cite: Ferretti, Jacopo et al. A two-stage strategy for methanogenesis suppression and rapid acetogenic biofilm formation in microbial electrosynthesis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2025.1655259. [PMC12616864]
growth_records.snippet: The HT reactor ... produced low amounts of butyrate and maintained lower acetate production rates (0.12 g L-1 d-1) throughout Stage 1 ... Reactor HT reached a significantly lower maximum acetate concentration of 0.8 g L-1.
growth_records.notes: Mixed undefined community, heterotrophic + heat-treated. Volumetric rates g/L or g/L/d; no biomass-specific conversion. mu ~0.01 d-1 continuous phase.

growth_records.strain: Reactor MT (heat-treated inoculum), Stage 2 autotrophic MES
growth_records.medium: MES phosphate-buffered mineral medium + trace elements, autotrophic (CO2 + current, no organic carbon)

up.method: sole carbon source, sparged; CO2 uptake not reported as molar rate

sec.method: GC-FID; autotrophic MES from CO2 + current

yields.note: Methane coulombic efficiency <1%; methane undetectable ~40 days

growth_records.cite: Ferretti, Jacopo et al. A two-stage strategy for methanogenesis suppression and rapid acetogenic biofilm formation in microbial electrosynthesis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2025.1655259. [PMC12616864]
growth_records.snippet: The MT reactor had a lag phase of only 1 day after the feeding regime switch, producing 5.1 g L-1 of acetate with a production rate of 1.1 g L-1 d-1, showing constant production for over 50 days.
growth_records.notes: Autotrophic (CO2 + electrons) acetogenesis by mixed community biofilm. Acetate 1.1 g/L/d volumetric. Applied current density -50 A/m2 (-10.1 kA/m3) is the electron uptake; CO2 uptake not reported as molar rate. No biomass-specific rate (biofilm).

growth_records.strain: Reactor HT (heat-treated inoculum), Stage 2 autotrophic MES
growth_records.medium: MES phosphate-buffered mineral medium + trace elements, autotrophic (CO2 + current, no organic carbon)

sec.units: g/L/d (constant to day 50, up to 5 g/L; then stable 4.7 g/L at 1.1 g/L/d)
sec.method: GC-FID; autotrophic MES from CO2 + current

growth_records.cite: Ferretti, Jacopo et al. A two-stage strategy for methanogenesis suppression and rapid acetogenic biofilm formation in microbial electrosynthesis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2025.1655259. [PMC12616864]
growth_records.snippet: Reactor HT ... began to produce acetate 6 days after the start of stage 2, with a relatively constant rate of 0.9 g L-1 d-1 until day 50, accumulating acetate up to 5 g L-1 ... a stable concentration of 4.7 g L-1, with a production rate of
growth_records.notes: Autotrophic acetogenesis (CO2 + electrons) by mixed community. Two reported acetate rate phases: 0.9 then 1.1 g/L/d. Electron uptake = -50 A/m2 (-10.1 kA/m3). No biomass-specific rate (biofilm).

growth_records.strain: Reactor H (untreated inoculum), Stage 2 autotrophic MES
growth_records.medium: MES phosphate-buffered mineral medium + trace elements, autotrophic (CO2 + current, no organic carbon)

sec.units: g/L/d (average production rate ~0; only sporadic low-level peaks)

growth_records.cite: Ferretti, Jacopo et al. A two-stage strategy for methanogenesis suppression and rapid acetogenic biofilm formation in microbial electrosynthesis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2025.1655259. [PMC12616864]
growth_records.snippet: However, it showed no carboxylate production: average production rates were close to zero, with variability caused by sporadic, low-level production peaks.
growth_records.notes: Negative/near-zero autotrophic acetate result for the heterotrophic-only-primed reactor; recorded as an experimental zero-rate condition. No biomass-specific rate.
